characters.name: Aegina
characters.description: AeginaJapanese NameアイギーナAigīnaSpeciesNanobyte androidGenderFemaleAffiliationsUnited Galaxy Space Force(formerly)BaglusFirst AppearanceShin-Gun Destroy! Girl's Tank Battalion(2014)Latest AppearanceShadow Labyrinth(2025) Aegina(アイギーナAigīna) is a character inShadow Labyrinthand a recurring character in the United Galaxy Space Force (UGSF) universe. She is an android originally created to serve in the Root Unit.[1] Aegina would eventually fulfill her purpose, joining the Root Unit under the division "Type Yellow".[2]One day, however, while partaking in an experiment involving a Dimension-Warp Drive, an accident occurred that left Aegina stranded on an unknown planet.[3]

characters.name: Alien Ghost
characters.description: Alien GhostHomelandLiver ofGhosteroidLeader(s)GhosteroidFirst AppearancePac-Man and the Ghostly AdventuresS1:E18 “Planet Pac” (2013)  Alien Ghostsare theghostsof an unknown alien species and many reside within the massiveGhosteroidwhich drifts through space. They are a species of extraterrestrial ghosts that shares a symbiotic relationship with Ghosteroids. The Ghosteroid shelters the Alien Ghosts, and in return, the Alien Ghosts protect it. Alien Ghosts first appeared in thePac-Man and the Ghostly Adventuresepisode, "Planet Pac". They show up during Pac's attempt to stop theGhosteroidfrom colliding withPac-World.

characters.name: Alley Cat
characters.description: Alley CatArtwork from a sticker sheet in a Pac-Man coloring book (1980s).SpeciesCatGenderUnknownHomelandPac-LandFirst AppearanceMr. & Mrs. Pac-Man(1982)Voiced ByNone Alley Cat(conjectural name) is a minor character in thePac-Manseries. First appearing on the artwork of theMr. & Mrs. Pac-Manpinball machine, the character would later appear in several pieces of1980s U.S. merchandising, such as the Pac-Man coloring books. While shown as a black, munchkin-like cat inMr. & Mrs. Pac-Man, the coloring books show the cat as a tabby.

characters.name: Alley Cat
characters.description: Alley CatArtwork from a sticker sheet in a Pac-Man coloring book (1980s).SpeciesCatGenderUnknownHomelandPac-LandFirst AppearanceMr. & Mrs. Pac-Man(1982)Voiced ByNone Alley Cat(conjectural name) is a minor character in thePac-Manseries. characters.name: Aloha
characters.description: "Attacks with Arrows from the other side of the book! Eat his eyes to make him smaller!" Alohais a boss fromPac-Pix. He attacks in Chapter 4 of Book 1. He uses arrows to destroy anyPac-Mandrawings on the field. The player must defeat Aloha using the Pac-Man drawings to attack Aloha's many eyes. After enough of Aloha's eyes are eaten, the ghost is defeated and the player learns to create Arrows. Aloha is a large plant-like creature with four eyes sticking out of his head and a spiky tendril. When the fight begins proper, he sprouts out six leaves which represent his health.

characters.name: Ancient Hero
characters.description: Ancient HeroSpeciesUnknownGenderMaleAffiliationsErwin(formerly)First AppearancePac-Man World 3Voiced ByPac-Man World 3:Garrick Hagon (all languages)Ancient Herois a character who appeared inPac-Man World 3. He is first encountered inCragstone Bridge, where he finally escaped fromErwin's Fortress. According to Erwin's dialogue inDungen Gunden, the Ancient Hero used to work for him as a janitor for a week before being let go. The Ancient Hero is a purple vaguely lizard-like humanoid creature, he has blue hair on both sides of his head and a beard of the same color. Ancient Hero is a deluded character who thinks and insists that he is a brave resistance fighter and fought aliens. He appears to be somewhat crazy given he believes that "burning eyes" are out to take his mind and seeing forces through the walls alongside boasting about his "heroic" deeds. He does not know much if anything as he himself does not know how he managed to get out of various areas. He does not appear to know how to handle dangerous situations as he spent an entire day learning the pattern of flame jets only to get toasted and merely annoyPac-Manwith his ramblings and overall uselessness. Although generally crazy, he does appear to be committed to his role of a janitor as he comments on the grime the floor and cleaning up messes sometimes.

characters.name: Apex
characters.description: ApexAffiliationsPointy HeadHomelandPlanet Pointy HeadFirst AppearancePac-Man and the Ghostly AdventuresVoiced ByPac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures:Colin Mourdock (English)Daisuke Namikawa (Japanese)Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures 2:Steve Pinto Colin Mourdock (English)Daisuke Namikawa (Japanese)Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures 2:Steve Pinto Apexis the leader of thePointy Headrace, a major antagonist inPac and the Ghostly Adventures, and he's responsible for hiring thePacinatorto commit genocide on the Yellow Ones. He first appears in Episode 24, "Robo Woes".

characters.description: ArachneJapanese NameArakuneアラクネSpeciesSpiderGenderMaleHomelandPac-Land,Mirage OasisFirst AppearancePac-Man Party(2010)Latest AppearancePac-Man Monsters(2014) アラクネ "Arachne! Set free after 1000 years! The flood must have broken the magic seal. If that thing goes on a rampage...No! I can't let that happen! I've got to protect the temple!"

characters.name: Astaroth
characters.description: AstarothArtwork from Soulcalibur II 2002).Japanese NameアスタロスAsutarosuGenderMaleFirst AppearanceSoulcalibur(1998, overall)Pac-Man Fever(2006, Pac-Man-related media)Latest AppearanceSoulcalibur VI(2018, overall)Pac-Man Fever(2006, Pac-Man-related media) Astaroth(アスタロスAsutarosu) is a character from Namco'sSoulcalibur(orSoul) series. He appears as a playable character inPac-Man Fever. Astaroth appears as one of the playable characters inPac-Man Fever, he first appears in the intro at theNamcoheadquarters where he chops wood with his axe in a metallic bus with his name on it. He alongside the rest of the playable characters then pursue a group of costumedghostsfor their trophy until they are all blocked off and forced to "buy all minigames".

characters.name: Astaroth
characters.description: AstarothArtwork from Soulcalibur II 2002).Japanese NameアスタロスAsutarosuGenderMaleFirst AppearanceSoulcalibur(1998, overall)Pac-Man Fever(2006, Pac-Man-related media)Latest AppearanceSoulcalibur VI(2018, overall)Pac-Man Fever(2006, Pac-Man-related media) Astaroth(アスタロスAsutarosu) is a character from Namco'sSoulcalibur(orSoul) series. characters.name: Aunt Spheria
characters.description: "Oh, I'll show you!" SpheriaisPac's aunt with a southern accent inPac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures. She served in thePac-World Waras a commando, while serving beside Pac's parents. She isSunny's sister. She is a very good Pac-Pong Player againstBetrayuswhileSpheroswas a coach/referee of Pac-Pong Fever in her flashback. She is known by her nickname in Pac-Pong, as "Spheria Suprema." With her expert skills, she teaches everybody inPac-Worldabout Pac-Pong, and she won in a rematch against Betrayus while Dr. Buttocks takes the chair. She is always withSir Cumferencein the series in his lab.

With her expert skills, she teaches everybody inPac-Worldabout Pac-Pong, and she won in a rematch against Betrayus while Dr. Buttocks takes the chair. She is always withSir Cumferencein the series in his lab.

characters.name: Baby Pac-Man
characters.description: Not to be confused forPac-Baby (Pac'N Roll). Baby Pac-ManArtwork from merchandising (1980s).Japanese NameパックベイビーPakku BeibīSpeciesPac-PersonGenderFemale (Pac-Man 2), Male in some earlier appearancesHomelandPac-LandFirst AppearanceMr. & Mrs. Pac-Man(1982, overall) (cameo)Baby Pac-Man(1982, proper)Latest AppearancePac-Man All-Stars(2002,Pac-Manseries) (under the Baby Pac-Man name)Pac-Man World 2 Re-Pac(2025,Pac-Manseries)(under the Pac-Sis name)Minecraft x Pac-Man(2020, other media)Voiced ByPac-ManTV Series:Russi Taylor (English)Frank Welker (English; grown-up) Pac-Man World 2 Re-Pac(2025,Pac-Manseries)(under the Pac-Sis name)Minecraft x Pac-Man(2020, other media) There are several characters in recent Pac-Man-related media that serve the same role as Baby Pac-Man, but feature different names and altered designs. These "replacements" of Baby Pac-Man were created due to legal issues regarding theMs. Pac-Manproperty; which, in turn, also effects Baby Pac-Man. For further information on this subject, seeHistory of Ms. Pac-Man legal issues.

Bashis aghostthat appeared exclusively in theSonic DashPAC-MAN Event. There he was a boss that would launch projectiles atPac-Man.

characters.name: Bearserk
characters.description: BearserkBearserk during the cutscene before he gets mad.SpeciesRobotic Teddy BearGenderMaleHomelandCelebration AveFirst AppearancePac-Man Party(2010)Voiced ByUnknown Bearserkis a robotic teddy bear that serves as the final boss inPac-Man Party. Bearserk appears in the fifth and final board,Celebration Avenue, as the boss of the stage. Bearserk starts out as a happy teddy bear who sits behind a wall. In front of the wall, there is a plate of jelly beans. As the players pass by the wall, they each take a jelly bean. Bearserk gradually gets more upset with each jelly bean they eat. When all the jelly beans are eaten, Bearserk grows enraged, revealing his robotic components, which include six sharp claws and quick-firing laser cannons located in his crimson eyes.

characters.name: Bearserk
characters.description: BearserkBearserk during the cutscene before he gets mad.SpeciesRobotic Teddy BearGenderMaleHomelandCelebration AveFirst AppearancePac-Man Party(2010)Voiced ByUnknown Bearserkis a robotic teddy bear that serves as the final boss inPac-Man Party. characters.name: Bero Bero
characters.description: "Good at warping, but because he doesn't know where will end up, he always gets lost." Bero BeroAffiliationsGhostHomelandGhost LandFirst AppearancePac-Pix Bero Bero(ベロベロBerobero) is a type of ghost that appears inPac-Pix. They are one of the many ghost types created fromGhost Ink. Once it gets chomped, Bero Beros will teleport away fromPac-Man, but as soon as they warp, they wander around disorientedly, and in this state Pac-Man can eat them successfully.

Once it gets chomped, Bero Beros will teleport away fromPac-Man, but as soon as they warp, they wander around disorientedly, and in this state Pac-Man can eat them successfully.

characters.name: Betrayus
characters.description: BetrayusArtwork fromPac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures(2013).Japanese NameビトレイアスBitoreiasuSpeciesFire GhostPac-Worlder(Formerly)GenderMaleHomelandNetherworldPac-World(formerly)First Appearance"Pac-Man (E3 2010 Pilot)" (2010)Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures(2013) S1:E1 "The Adventure Begins, Part I"Latest AppearancePac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures 2Voiced ByPac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures:Samuel Vincent (English)Tsuguo Mogami (Japanese)Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures (video game):Chistopher Corey Smith (English) Pac-Worlder(Formerly) Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures(2013) S1:E1 "The Adventure Begins, Part I" Aside from being evil, incompetent, and power-hungry, Betrayus is very egotistical and selfish, always ready to take full credit for all of his evil plans, always ready to call himself the best at something, and always ready to claim something for himself. He has no tolerance for failure from his minions and generally mistreats them, most notably Butt-ler. Betrayus is also very cowardly, letting others do the dirty work for him. He is additionally very childish, as he tends to throw tantrums whenever things don't go his way, has tendency to blame others, and is unable to sleep without his stuffed toy[1]. His greedy desires give him an insatiable lust for power and will do just about anything to get his way and become stronger. During his time as leader of his army when he was alive, he was a childish, but still strict, and competent leader who inspired genuine fear and loyalty in his troops and led successful attacks on the Resistance, although he would still become somewhat cowardly when he was outmatched or throw temper tantrums as a result. However, after he was stripped of his body and banished to the Netherworld, he became a childish overlord who inspires very little respect or admiration in others and is only able to get his way through threats and intimidation. Even his own ghost forces aren't particularly fond of him. However, Betrayus is clever enough to spot an opportunity to strike a blow to his enemies whenever it comes up. Betrayus is so impossible to deal with, he couldn't even get along with his own clone, who mocked and disrespected him constantly during its short period of existence.[2]

characters.name: Biggaboo
characters.description: "Naturally, he believes when it comes to size, the bigger the better!" "Somehow he managed to fly! He just aimed for the sky and said, "I can do it!"" "Wrapped in armor, this guy is almost invincible. Seems he hasn't heard of Bombs yet..."

characters.name: Biggaboo
characters.description: "Naturally, he believes when it comes to size, the bigger the better!" "Somehow he managed to fly! He just aimed for the sky and said, "I can do it!"" "Wrapped in armor, this guy is almost invincible. Seems he hasn't heard of Bombs yet..."

characters.name: Blasty
characters.description: BlastyIcon from the Custom Menu ofPac-Man Party RoyaleSpeciesGhostGenderUnknownHomelandGhost LandFirst AppearancePac-Man Party Royale(2024) Blasty(ブラスティ Blasty) is anGhostfromPac-Man Party Royale. They are an orange Ghost who wears a bomb as a mask. They were added in update 4.5.0 of the game.[1] Blasty works similarly toItchyin that they inflict a negative status effect onPac-Maninstead of killing him outright. Blasty has only one effect, which is to transfer their bomb onto the Pac-Man that touches them. After a few seconds pass, the bomb will explode, instantly killing the Pac-Man that was wearing it.

Blasty has only one effect, which is to transfer their bomb onto the Pac-Man that touches them. After a few seconds pass, the bomb will explode, instantly killing the Pac-Man that was wearing it.

characters.name: Blinky
characters.description: BlinkyRegularRegular (3D)ArcadeArcade (3D)Pac-Man PartyGhostly AdventuresJapanese Name追いかけOikake(Character, 1980 & Arrangement AC)アカベイAkabei(Nickname, 1980, Arrangement AC & Mario Kart Arcade GP)ブリンキーBurinkii(1984-present)SpeciesGhostGenderMaleHomelandGhost LandFirst AppearancePac-Man(1980)Latest AppearancePac-Man World 2 Re-Pac(2025)Voiced ByPac-Man TV Series:Chuck McCann (English)Pac-Man World 2:Hikaru Midorikawa (Japanese)Pac-Man World 3:John Guerrasio (all languages)Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures:Ian James Corlett (English, TV series)Lucien Dodge (English, video games)Kazuhiro Fusegawa (Japanese)Pac-Man World 2 Re-Pac:Alejandro Saab (all languages) "Aw, come onMunchy! You don't wanna eat me. Think of all we've been through together. Okay, bad example." Blinky(ブリンキーBurinkii), also known asAkabeiin Japanese and nicknamedShadow, is the leader of the Ghosts and the arch-enemy ofPac-Man. He is also portrayed as the most aggressive ghost who always chases Pac-Man, and is hard to shake once he starts. He is known to have a short temper, and is good friends withPinky,Inky, andClyde. He also has a daughter namedYum-Yum. Blinky is a red-colored ghost. He has big eyes, and often has an angry expression. He is typically limbless within games, but some incarnations show him with arms, sometimes with orange gloves as well. In the TV series andPac-Land, Blinky wears a wide-brimmed hat, and has a somewhat cowardly and afraid expression rather than being angry. InPac-Man PartyandGhostly Adventures, he has a protrusion to the top of his head (which he calls his "hairdo") and blue eyes; his arms are also stubbier. Blinky is the most determined of the ghosts, and the leader of the Ghost Gang. He is often depicted as the most intelligent and most conceited of the quartet. He is also the most aggressive ghost in all "maze-oriented"Pac-Mangames, relentlessly chasing Pac-Man/Ms. Pac-Man. Though Pac-Man (under most circumstances) is an enemy of all of the ghosts, Blinky is his main nemesis among them; however, some games depict this as a form of friendly antagonism. In the Hanna-Barbera series, Blinky is considered shy, cowardly and slow-witted. He is often reluctant to being dragged in Clyde and Mezmaron's schemes, mostly out of fear that it will go wrong. Neverthenless, he still joins in because he has no choice. Blinky's cowardice is most often shown when Pac-Man has just eaten a Power Pellet and Blinky tries to run away to avoid being chomped by Pac-Man, albeit to no avail. Blinky is also shown to have grammar problems on occasion, though his grammatical problems are a result of his cowardice. He tends to surrender and admit defeat when in a defenseless position (especially if he is the only remaining Ghost Monster), and sometimes undergo a nervous breakdown in the process, or even "undressing" himself into just his remaining, floating eyes, effectively doing the job for Pac-Man. InPac-Man World 2andPac-Man World 3, Blinky has less angry expression. He later

characters.name: Blue
characters.description: "He's the fastest, but not he's not strong over long distances." Blueis an enemy fromPac-Pix. Although he appears similar toInky, he is called Blue on his Card. He is the second most common enemy found, and uses his speed to dodge oncomingPac-Mandrawings. However, his speed only allows him to go so far, he eventually tires himself out and leaves him susceptible to attack. Blue makes his sole appearance in the game Pac-Pix. He is a common enemy who can evade the Pac-Man drawings by his speed but tires himself after doing so a few times; he can also appear on the top screen but is unable to dash on there, leaving him vulnerable to any Pac-Man drawings. He can additionally appear within bubbles that must be popped by arrows so he can be eaten.

characters.name: Bouncy Pac-Man
characters.description: Bouncy Pac-Manis one ofPac-Man's friends and one of the variousPac-Peopleheld hostage inPac-Man Friends. He resembles a beach ball and much like the object he is based on, he is very bouncy and can rebound much more easily off of walls compared to the other Pac-People. Bouncy Pac-Man makes his sole appearance in Pac-Man Friends as one of the various Pac-People Pac-Man rescues during his journey. He first appears in level 23 and makes more appearances throughout the rest of the game. The difference in bounce power between Bouncy Pac-Man andPac-Man

characters.name: Bull-Dog
characters.description: Bull-DogSpeciesDogHomelandPac-LandFirst AppearancePac-Man 2: The New Adventures(1994) Bull-Dog[1]is a character only appearing inPac-Man 2: The New Adventures. In the game, he sleeps in his doghouse outside ofLucy's house, in whichPac-Manslinks by not to wake him up. If Pac-Man wakes him up, Bull-Dog's barking scares him, in which he grabs onto a tree branch out of fear. Afterwards, the tree turns into a slot roulette, and upon winning, apples fall down the tree, causing Bull-Dog to run back into his doghouse out of fear. Upon losing, the branch Pac-Man grabs onto breaks, and Bull-Dog mauls him as a result.

characters.name: Butt-ler
characters.description: Butt-lerAffiliationsGhostPac-Worlder(Formerly)GenderMaleHomelandNetherworldLeader(s)BetrayusFirst AppearancePac-Man and the Ghostly AdventuresS1:E1 "The Adventure Begins Part I" (2013)Voiced ByBrian Drummond (EN)Shūhei Takubo (JP)[1] "Perhaps a bit of good old-fashioned mayhem will take the sting out of this situation,sir. There is an invasion to command." Butt-lerisBetrayus' long suffering personal servant and butler, and the twin brother ofDr. Buttocks. He is one of the main villains inPac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures. He is a purple-gray ghost with a butt-shaped forehead (as his name implies), topped with a dapper black hat. He is always devoted to his master. He will, however, take some satisfaction in seeing Betrayus fall flat on his face (which happens a lot). He never passes up an opportunity to make fun of his twin brother. When speaking to his brother and Betrayus, he can be passive aggressive at times. He has a pseudo-friendly relationship withPacand the others - they generally hold a "live and let live" attitude towards him, being aware he doesn't share his brother's malicious manner.

characters.name: Buttercup
characters.description: ButtercupArtwork of Buttercup fromPac-Land(1984)SpeciesFairyGenderFemaleHomelandFairylandFirst AppearancePac-Land(1984)Latest AppearancePac-Man Pinball(2003) "Pac-Manis back forhis most dangerous mission yet.  He must avoiddeadly ghostsand perilous obstacles to help the beautiful fairy princess Buttercup find her way throughPac-Land." Buttercup, the beautifulfairyprincess[1]is a fairy who first appeared inPac-Land.

characters.name: Captain
characters.description: CaptainSpeciesPac-PersonGenderMaleHomelandPac-LandFirst AppearancePac-Mantelevision series, "The Old Pac-Man and the Sea"Voiced ByBill Callaway "You remember what John Pac-Jones said: "Don't pack up the ship"!" TheCaptainis aPac-Personwho appears in the episode "The Old Pac-Man and the Sea" of thePac-Man TV show. He is an elderly Pac-Person who owns a ship and went withPac-MantoPaclantisafter their ship sinks.

characters.name: Captain Tentacle
characters.description: "Uh oh! That's Captain Tentacle! The meanest octopus inPacworld!" Captain TentacleSpeciesOctopusGenderMaleHomelandPac-Land,Greenwood GroveFirst AppearancePac-Man Party Captain Tentacleis a giant octopus that resides thePac-Worldand is an enemy ofPac-Man. He is an ambitious sort, as he wants to be a legend, like Kraken. He is summoned by the croaking of a group offrogs. He appears inPac-Man Party.

characters.name: Cassie the Cow
characters.description: Cassie the Cow[1]is a minor character inPac-Man 2: The New Adventures. She appears behind a fence in the farm. IfPac-Manwalks near Cassie, he will stop to look at her, which improves his mood. If the player shoots her with the slingshot, she will angrily swat Pac-Man with her tail, knocking him out. Once the nearby glass bottle is knocked down off of the sign, Pac-Man can use it to milk Cassie. Doing this is the objective for the first mission of the game.
